name: Ricobayo Reservoir and Tierra de Alba Loop
name_original: Circular por la Tierra de Alba y el Embalse de Ricobayo — por la ermita de la Virgen del Castillo
description: This moderate 30.0-mile road cycling route offers scenic views of the Ricobayo reservoir and the ermita de la Virgen del Castillo.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Manzanal Del Barco traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Zamora, Spain. The region is characterized by the expansive Ricobayo Reservoir, formed by the Esla River, offering scenic views along its shores. Cyclists will encounter varying terrains, from undulating hills to areas with significant elevation gains, providing options for different fitness levels. Proximity to the Arribes del Duero Natural Park also contributes to the area's natural features, including deep river gorges and large groves of oaks. Best road cycling routes around Manzanal Del Barco The most popular road cycling route is Ricobayo Reservoir and Tierra de Alba Loop, a 30.0 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ricobayo Reservoir Loop, a moderate 41.8 miles (67.3 km) path. This route features varying terrains and scenic vistas around the reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Ricobayo Reservoir Loop, a 45.3 miles (73.0 km) trail leading through areas with significant elevation gains, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Manzanal Del Barco is defined by the Ricobayo Reservoir, undulating hills, and diverse landscapes. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Manzanal Del Barco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Manzanal Del Barco?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrains, from undulating hills to areas with significant elevation gains, especially around the Ricobayo Reservoir. You'll find routes suitable for various fitness levels, with options for both moderate and more challenging rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Manzanal Del Barco area?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Manzanal Del Barco. These include 16 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What are some key natural features I'll encounter while road cycling here?
A: A central feature is the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/strong&gt;, formed by the Esla River, offering scenic views along its shores. The area is also close to the &lt;strong&gt;Arribes del Duero Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its deep river gorges and rich biodiversity. You'll also see large groves of oaks and poplars, and undulating hills.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Manzanal Del Barco?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369316545/ricobayo-reservoir-and-tierra-de-alba-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir and Tierra de Alba Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route offering diverse landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: You can explore several notable attractions. Consider visiting the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de la Nave&lt;/a&gt;, a historically significant church relocated stone by stone. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant engineering marvel with spectacular views. You might also pass by the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3024773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Manzanal Del Barco?
A: The spring and autumn seasons are particularly recommended for road cycling in Manzanal Del Barco. During these times, you can expect pleasant temperatures, which are ideal for outdoor pursuits and enjoying the scenic routes.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir – Requejo Bridge loop from Central Hidroelectrica Ricobayo&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 900 meters of ascent over 66 kilometers.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Manzanal Del Barco?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Manzanal Del Barco. Over 140 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and the diverse landscapes encountered on the routes.

Q: Can I find shorter, moderate road cycling routes suitable for a less intense ride?
A: Yes, there are many moderate options. For a ride around 40 kilometers, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38143849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir – Ricobayo Dam loop from Central Hidroelectrica Ricobayo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views with a manageable elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I find near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the Ricobayo Reservoir, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilares del Pan Beach&lt;/a&gt; for a relaxing stop, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6118308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Manzanal Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for the nearby La Palia Waterfall, a natural attraction for nature lovers.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically showcase the Ricobayo Reservoir?
A: Many routes are designed to highlight the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1369324841/ricobayo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering varying terrains and extensive scenic vistas around the water.
tips: To the north of the Sierra de Cantadores we find this mountain range that is located along the banks of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Beautiful area of crowded areas.

The Ricobayo reservoir and the hydroelectric dam were created by capturing water from the Esla River. In addition to producing power, the reservoir is also a popular recreational location with many beaches and water sports activities.

The monumental heritage is present even in the small Castilian towns such as Montamarta. The hermitage dates from the 12th century and when the reservoir is full its figure is reflected in the water, accentuating its ancestral and bucolic vision.

Bridges that cross the Esla river. Inaugurated in 2007, the new Manzanal del Barco bridge left in ruins the old and narrow bridge built during the construction of the Ricobayo Reservoir over the Esla River in 1931 until it was finally closed to traffic in 2017.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route, covering approximately 48.3 kilometers with about 511 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 2 hours and 10 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and stops along the way.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as moderate, meaning it requires a good level of fitness. While it's primarily for road cycling, the distance and elevation gain suggest it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Cyclists should be comfortable with rides up to 5 hours or with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, so you can start and park in various locations. A common starting point would be near Perilla de Castro or other villages along the route. Specific parking information for the start of the loop is not detailed, but roadside parking in the villages is generally available.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for road cycling, so you can expect mostly paved surfaces. However, as a moderate route in a natural area, it's possible some segments, especially closer to viewpoints, might involve less-maintained or slightly unpaved sections. The general area features both well-maintained roads and varying terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in Zamora, and winters might bring colder, potentially wet conditions. These seasons offer pleasant weather for enjoying the scenic views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect spectacular views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and the impressive Ricobayo Dam. The landscape features typical Zamorano environments, including cistus, holm oaks, cereal fields, and pastures. The route also offers panoramic views from points like the Views of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the expansive Ricobayo Reservoir and the Ricobayo Dam, a significant hydraulic engineering feat. You'll also pass through Perilla de Castro, enjoy the Views of the Hermitage of the Virgen del Castillo, and see The Three Bridges of Manzanal del Barco.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with a few other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Mtb Vía de la Plata (5% of the route), Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga (5%), Camino del Monte (2% and 1%), Calle El Piñedo (1%), and Camino del Espadañal (1%).

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in public outdoor spaces in Spain, this is a road cycling route. Bringing a dog would typically require them to be in a trailer or basket designed for cycling, or to be left at home for their safety and comfort on a moderate-difficulty road ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths around the Ricobayo Reservoir and through the Tierra de Alba region.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through villages like Perilla de Castro, you may find small cafes or bars for refreshments. For more extensive options or accommodation, nearby towns in the Tierra de Alba region would be your best bet.
